# ADR-009 Compliance — Deployment & Operations

**Governance phase:** 3.5 · **Authority document:** `docs/ADR-009-*.md` · **Date:** 2026-07-19
**Verifier:** `architecture/compliance/verify-architecture.mjs` · **Do not edit the ADR itself.**

## Purpose
Define how the platform is built, shipped, deployed, rolled back, backed up, and operated.

## Scope
Enterprise operations architecture — deployment topology, CI/CD, backup/DR.

## Current implementation status
**Proposed; Infrastructure present, cutover pending — 55% compliant.**

## Implemented components
- Dockerfile + backup Dockerfile
- docker-compose.prod.yml (app + postgres:17 + redis:7 + nginx TLS edge)
- 6 CI/CD workflows (ci, deploy, docker-release, emergency-rollback, quality, release-please)
- Backup compose + backup service (WAL checkpoint, retention)

## Remaining work
- App consuming Postgres/Redis (forward-provisioned, not wired)
- Offsite backup storage
- Per-context deploy boundaries (only if load justifies — G0.0)

## Evidence
- Dockerfile (85L), compose.prod (188L), nginx.conf (89L)
- 6 workflow files
- src/services/backup.js live backup

## Related source code
- `Dockerfile`
- `docker-compose.prod.yml`
- `nginx/nginx.conf`
- `.github/workflows/*`

## Related tests
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

## Compliance percentage
**55%** — honest estimate: weight of ADR requirements realized under the ADR-005
layered architecture with evidence, vs. total requirements named in the ADR.

## Risks
- Backups are local disk (no offsite) — DR gap
- Postgres/Redis provisioned but unused → single-instance runtime

## Required actions
- Wire Redis for rate-limit/cache/revocation (G5)
- Add offsite backup target
